input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a complete example:

**XAML**
```xml
<CollectionView
    ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem, Mode=TwoWay}"
    SelectionMode="Single"
    IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}"
    RemainingItemsThreshold="0">

    <CollectionView.Behaviors>
        <toolkit:EventToCommandBehavior
            EventName="SelectionChanged"
            Command="{Binding ItemSelectedCommand}" />
    </CollectionView.Behaviors>

    <!-- Pull-to-refresh requires wrapping in a RefreshView -->
</CollectionView>
```

`CollectionView` doesn't natively support pull-to-refresh — wrap it in a **`RefreshView`**:

```xml
<RefreshView
    Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
    IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">

    <CollectionView
        ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
        SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem, Mode=TwoWay}"
        SelectionMode="Single">

        <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate x:DataType="models:MyItem">
                <Grid Padding="12" ColumnDefinitions="*,Auto">
                    <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="16" />
                    <Label Grid.Column="1" Text="{Binding Status}"
                           TextColor="Gray" />
                </Grid>
            </DataTemplate>
        </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
    </CollectionView>
</RefreshView>
```

**ViewModel**
```csharp
public partial class ItemsViewModel : ObservableObject
{
    private readonly IApiService _api;
    private readonly INavigationService _nav;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private ObservableCollection<MyItem> _items = [];

    [ObservableProperty]
    private MyItem? _selectedItem;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private bool _isRefreshing;

    // Fires on pull-to-refresh
    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task RefreshAsync()
    {
        try
        {
            var data = await _api.GetItemsAsync();
            Items = new ObservableCollection<MyItem>(data);
        }
        finally
        {
            IsRefreshing = false;   // stops the spinner
        }
    }

    // Fires when selection changes
    partial void OnSelectedItemChanged(MyItem? value)
    {
        if (value is null) return;
        _nav.NavigateAsync("DetailPage", new { Id = value.Id });
        SelectedItem = null;        // reset so the same row is re-selectable
    }
}
```

**Key binding points:**

| XAML Property | ViewModel Property | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `ItemsSource` | `Items` (ObservableCollection) | Data source |
| `SelectedItem` *(TwoWay)* | `SelectedItem` | Tracks selection; set to `null` after navigation to allow reselection |
| `RefreshView.Command` | `RefreshCommand` | Triggered on pull-down gesture |
| `RefreshView.IsRefreshing` *(TwoWay)* | `IsRefreshing` | Set `false` when load completes to dismiss the spinner |
| `SelectionMode="Single"` | — | Restricts to one selected item at a time |

**Important notes:**
- `IsRefreshing` must be set to `false` in a `finally` block, otherwise the spinner hangs on errors.
- Reset `SelectedItem = null` after navigating so tapping the same row again still triggers navigation.
- Uses **CommunityToolkit.Mvvm** source generators (`[ObservableProperty]`, `[RelayCommand]`).
